methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Duranium is a pre-seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Berkley Noble and Brenden Prins-McKinney that has raised $2M+. The Climate & Energy sector includes nine private peers and one public comparable (LNZA), though meaningful direct peer valuation data at this stage remains sparse.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method here. Using the $2M total raised as a base value proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of pre-seed to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $5M. This reflects observed market dynamics for companies moving from pre-seed funding rounds. Peer-median analysis is not applicable; while the sector contains nine comparable companies—including later-stage peers like Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen—none share Duranium's combination of stage, funding tier, and timeline, yielding insufficient datapoints for reliable triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $4M to $6M, with a median of $5M. This single-method conclusion carries meaningful uncertainty around stage timing and capital efficiency relative to peer trajectories. Key risks to valuation include execution risk on next funding round and sector-specific capital availability in climate tech. The range should be revisited once additional comparable transactions or peer financing data becomes available.
